channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

item.title: NASA says meteor that landed in Cape Cod considered to be made of iron - Yahoo
item.description:
1. NASA says meteor that landed in Cape Cod considered to be made of iron  Yahoo
  2. Meteor that landed in Cape Cod Bay may have been made of iron and was unusually dense, NASA says  The Boston Globe
  3. Meteor explodes off coast of Massachusetts, causing loud boom  CBS News
  4. On a Saturday afternoon in May 2026, a rock about three feet across hit the atmosphere over New England at 75,000 mph and broke apart with the energy of roughly 300 tons of TNT, and the boom carried from Delaware to Montreal, farther than any fragment e  Space Daily
  5. Massive boom over northeastern US was a meteor explosion as powerful as 300 tons of TNT, NASA confirms  Space

item.title: Scientists say the gold in wedding rings, teeth, and family heirlooms was forged in cosmic catastrophes long before our sun existed - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Scientists say the gold in wedding rings, teeth, and family heirlooms was forged in cosmic catastrophes long before our sun existed  Space Daily
  2. Almost all the gold on Earth — every wedding ring, every coin, every gram in every bank vault — was forged in the collision of dead stars billions of years ago, in events so violent that a single one can produce hundreds of Earth-masses of gold, and the heavy  Space Daily

item.title: The asteroid that ended the dinosaurs did not just leave a crater, it turned the sky into a weapon, and the worst damage may have come after the impact itself was already over - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The asteroid that ended the dinosaurs did not just leave a crater, it turned the sky into a weapon, and the worst damage may have come after the impact itself was already over  Space Daily
  2. When The Dinosaur-Killing Asteroid Hit, This Life-Form Feasted On The Death  ScienceAlert
  3. Ancient findings reveal dinosaurs faced a hidden crisis before the infamous asteroid hit  Yahoo
  4. Extinction: What were conditions like on Earth when the dinosaur-killing asteroid crashed into the planet?  FOX Weather
  5. Story of dinosaur-killer meteorite impact told in vivid detail  The Guardian

item.title: Fossils found in Scotland just pushed back the origin of land-walking animals by 14 million years and placed them in a stretch of the fossil record where nothing was supposed to exist - Space Daily
item.description: Fossils found in Scotland just pushed back the origin of land-walking animals by 14 million years and placed them in a stretch of the fossil record where nothing was supposed to exist  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists say the oxygen you just breathed in was once the deadliest poison on the planet, released by tiny microbes that accidentally wiped out most of the life around them in what geologists call the Great Oxidation Event - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Scientists say the oxygen you just breathed in was once the deadliest poison on the planet, released by tiny microbes that accidentally wiped out most of the life around them in what geologists call the Great Oxidation Event  Space Daily
  2. One of the earliest great extinctions in Earth's history may have been caused not by an asteroid or a volcano but by oxygen itself, when tiny photosynthetic microbes slowly filled the air with a gas that was poison to much of the anaerobic life that ruled the planet  Space Daily
  3. Want an oxygen-rich atmosphere? Stuff oxygen’s friends in the mantle.  Ars Technica
  4. Earth's oxygen-rich atmosphere may owe its existence to cold subduction  Phys.org
  5. The oxygen in Earth's atmosphere is essential to almost every animal alive today — but when it first started accumulating in the air roughly 2.4 billion years ago, it triggered the most lethal pollution event in the planet's history, wiping out the vast majority of spec  Space Daily

item.title: The Pacific Ocean is so large that all the world’s land could fit inside it, and there would still be room left over, which is why calling Earth a “blue planet” is almost an understatement - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Pacific Ocean is so large that all the world’s land could fit inside it, and there would still be room left over, which is why calling Earth a “blue planet” is almost an understatement  Space Daily
  2. The Oceans Of The World By Size  WorldAtlas
  3. The Pacific Ocean is so vast that every continent, every island, and every desert on Earth could fit inside it with room to spare, which is why calling our planet blue is almost an understatement  Space Daily
  4. The Pacific Ocean is so vast that it's larger than every continent on Earth combined — and there's a single straight line you could sail through it for nearly 20,000 miles without ever touching land  Space Daily

item.title: A tongue-eating louse called Cymothoa exigua swims into a fish's gills, latches onto its tongue, drinks the blood until the tongue withers and falls off, and then spends the rest of its life acting as a functional replacement tongue the fish uses normally to eat. - Space Daily
